query:
You are working on the ankidroid/Anki-Android repository.
The codebase is at version 2.175 (commit: 05a5e229).

## Issue to Resolve

[BUG]: Edit Note Preview does not show the expected card
### Checked for duplicates?

- [X] This issue is not a duplicate

### What are the steps to reproduce this bug?

open the browser
Select a card which is not card 1 of the note
long press
in the menu, tap "edit note"
Tap preview

### Expected behaviour

You should see the selected card in the preview mode (or at least, the card should be consistent with the card underlined at the bottom of the editor)

### Actual behaviour

First card of the note is shown (At least I assume. In the case I was interested in, I saw the "this card is empty" warning)

### Debug info

```text
2.17 alpha 3
```


### (Optional) Anything else you want to share?

_No response_

### Research

- [X] I am reporting a bug specific to AnkiDroid (Android app)
- [X] I have checked the [manual](https://ankidroid.org/docs/manual.html) and the [FAQ](https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/wiki/FAQ) and could not find a solution to my issue
- [ ] (Optional) I have confirmed the issue is not resolved in the latest alpha release ([instructions](https://docs.ankidroid.org/manual.html#betaTesting))

## Additional Context from Issue Discussion

This will be slightly easier to fix once https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/pull/14719 goes in:

* `ordinal` is provided to the editor: 
* `ordinal` is `ord`, NOT the index into the array
  * `{{c1::AA}} {{c3::BB}}` returns the second ordinal as 2

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L84

`mOrdinal` is not used to set `templateIndex` in this branch:

https://github.com/ankidroid/Anki-Android/blob/170c2d1ee5a354b42395e8c0de4363a1e1fc367b/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt#L233-L241

`setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` uses `templateIndex = 0` and displays the wrong card

I've added two regression tests to help guide a fix

One undefined question: what should the note editor previewer show if one of the cards is empty? Should it be skipped, or displayed

Should this be different between:
* Card Template Preview
* Note Editor Preview
* Card Browser Preview (less likely to want to see empty cards)

For when a few dependencies have been merged:

```kotlin
    private fun indexFromOrdinal(col: Collection, ordinal: Int): Int {
        return when (mEditedNotetype?.isCloze) {
            true -> col.clozeNumbersInNote(col.newNote(mEditedNotetype!!)).indexOf(mOrdinal)
            else -> ordinal
        }
    }
```
Is that correct? with c1 and c3, that will return [1,3], and ord 3 will be an index out of bounds, won't it?

Edit: sorry, ignore me - I mistook this for numbers[ord] instead of searching for the given value.
In the note editor preview, there seems to be no specific card. So I don’t understand what sense it would make to have this behavior on the note editor preview.

For card template preview, I certainly believe you should see the preview for the card you are looking it.

For the browser, if the card selected in the browser is empty, I believe you should show that the card is empty. That’s an information I certainly may want to have if it is the case. Because either:
* there was no recent "clean card"
* there was a clear card, and this is card 1, generated in order to ensure the note is not deleted. 

The first case may be interesting, the second is certainly something that should be noted.

Also, if we touch this part of the code, it may be worth to give more context, by stating either:
* This card is empty. The n cards of this note are empty.
* This card is empty. Jump to the first non-empty card. (with a link/button)

And I guess, potentially, a checkbox to skip the empty card when browsing which cards will be generated.


If you agree it makes sense, I assume it should be many different bugs so they can be easily tackled

## Relevant Files

The following files are likely relevant to solving this issue:
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt`
- `AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/libanki/Collection.kt`

## Instructions

Please analyze this issue and implement a fix. Make the necessary code changes to resolve the problem described above.

Focus on:
- Understanding the root cause of the issue
- Making minimal, targeted changes to fix the problem
- Ensuring your changes don't break existing functionality

## Important Evaluation Notes

**Your work will be evaluated by running hidden tests that you do not have access to.** These tests will verify that your fix correctly addresses the issue. You should be confident in your solution before finishing.
**If you write tests to verify your changes, create them in NEW test files only** - do NOT modify existing test files

## CRITICAL RESTRICTIONS

**NEVER use git commands.** Do not run `git status`, `git diff`, `git checkout`, `git reset`, or ANY other git commands. Using git will corrupt the evaluation environment and invalidate your entire submission. All file operations should be done through normal file editing and reading tools, not git.

 43→    @Test
 44→    fun testPreviewUnsavedTemplate() {
 45→        val modelName = "Basic"
 46→        val collectionBasicModelOriginal = getCurrentDatabaseModelCopy(modelName)
 47→        val template = collectionBasicModelOriginal.getJSONArray("tmpls").getJSONObject(0)
 48→        template.put("qfmt", template.getString("qfmt") + "PREVIEWER_TEST")
 49→        val tempModelPath = CardTemplateNotetype.saveTempModel(targetContext, collectionBasicModelOriginal)
 50→        val intent = Intent(Intent.ACTION_VIEW)
 51→        intent.putExtra(CardTemplateNotetype.INTENT_MODEL_FILENAME, tempModelPath)
 52→        intent.putExtra("index", 0)
 53→
 54→        var previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java, intent).create().start().resume().visible()
 55→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
 56→        var testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
 57→        Assert.assertTrue(
 58→            "model change did not show up?",
 59→            testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.q().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST") &&
 60→                testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.a().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST")
 61→        )
 62→
 63→        // Take it through a destroy/re-create lifecycle in order to test instance state persistence
 64→        val outBundle = Bundle()
 65→        previewerController.saveInstanceState(outBundle)
 66→        previewerController.pause().stop().destroy()
 67→        previewerController = Robolectric.buildActivity(TestCardTemplatePreviewer::class.java).create(outBundle).start().resume().visible()
 68→        saveControllerForCleanup(previewerController)
 69→        testCardTemplatePreviewer = previewerController.get()
 70→        Assert.assertTrue(
 71→            "model change not preserved in lifecycle??",
 72→            testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.q().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST") &&
 73→                testCardTemplatePreviewer.getDummyCard(collectionBasicModelOriginal, 0)!!.a().contains("PREVIEWER_TEST")
 74→        )
 75→
 76→        // Make sure we can click
 77→        Assert.assertFalse("Showing the answer already?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
 78→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.disableDoubleClickPrevention()
 79→        val showAnswerButton = testCardTemplatePreviewer.findViewById<View>(R.id.preview_buttons_layout)
 80→        showAnswerButton.performClick()
 81→        Assert.assertTrue("Not showing the answer?", testCardTemplatePreviewer.showingAnswer)
 82→    }

409→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
410→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
411→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
412→
413→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onNextCard()
414→
415→        // no effect
416→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
417→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
418→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
419→
420→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onPreviousCard()
421→
422→        // previous
423→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(0))
424→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
425→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
426→    }
427→
428→    private fun getSavedCard(notetype: NotetypeJson, ordinal: Int): Card {
429→        val n = col.newNote(notetype)
430→        val fieldNames = notetype.fieldsNames
431→        for (i in fieldNames.indices) {
432→            n.setField(i, fieldNames[i])
433→        }
434→        col.addNote(n)
435→        print(ordinal)
436→        return n.cards()[0]
437→    }
438→
439→    @KotlinCleanup("Override fieldText in constructor and remove text")
440→    private inner class Field(override val ord: Int, private val text: String) : NoteService.NoteField {
441→        override val fieldText: String
442→            get() = text
443→    }
444→}

 70→open class RobolectricTest : AndroidTest {
 71→
 72→    @Suppress("PLATFORM_CLASS_MAPPED_TO_KOTLIN")
 73→    private fun Any.wait(timeMs: Long) = (this as Object).wait(timeMs)
 74→
 75→    private val mControllersForCleanup = ArrayList<ActivityController<*>>()
 76→
 77→    protected fun saveControllerForCleanup(controller: ActivityController<*>) {
 78→        mControllersForCleanup.add(controller)
 79→    }
 80→
 81→    protected open fun useInMemoryDatabase(): Boolean {
 82→        return true
 83→    }
 84→
 85→    @get:Rule
 86→    val mTaskScheduler = TaskSchedulerRule()
 87→
 88→    /** Allows [com.ichi2.testutils.Flaky] to annotate tests in subclasses */
 89→    @get:Rule
 90→    val ignoreFlakyTests = IgnoreFlakyTestsInCIRule()
 91→
 92→    @Before
 93→    @CallSuper
 94→    open fun setUp() {
 95→        TimeManager.resetWith(MockTime(2020, 7, 7, 7, 0, 0, 0, 10))
 96→
 97→        ChangeManager.clearSubscribers()
 98→
 99→        // resolved issues with the collection being reused if useInMemoryDatabase is false
100→        CollectionHelper.instance.setColForTests(null)
101→
102→        maybeSetupBackend()
103→
104→        // If you want to see the Android logging (from Timber), you need to set it up here
105→        ShadowLog.stream = System.out
106→
107→        Storage.setUseInMemory(useInMemoryDatabase())
108→
109→        // Reset static variable for custom tabs failure.
110→        CustomTabActivityHelper.resetFailed()
111→
112→        // See: #6140 - This global ideally shouldn't exist, but it will cause crashes if set.
113→        DialogHandler.discardMessage()
114→
115→        // BUG: We do not reset the MetaDB
116→        MetaDB.closeDB()
117→    }
118→
119→    protected open fun useLegacyHelper(): Boolean {
120→        return false
121→    }
122→
123→    protected fun getHelperFactory(): SupportSQLiteOpenHelper.Factory =
124→        if (useInMemoryDatabase()) {
125→            Timber.w("Using in-memory database for test. Collection should not be re-opened")
126→            InMemorySQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
127→        } else {
128→            FrameworkSQLiteOpenHelperFactory()
129→        }
130→
131→    @After
132→    @CallSuper
133→    open fun tearDown() {
134→        // If you don't clean up your ActivityControllers you will get OOM errors
135→        for (controller in mControllersForCleanup) {
136→            Timber.d("Calling destroy on controller %s", controller.get().toString())
137→            try {
138→                controller.destroy()
139→            } catch (e: Exception) {
140→                // Any exception here is likely because the test code already destroyed it, which is fine
141→                // No exception here should halt test execution since tests are over anyway.
142→            }
143→        }
144→        mControllersForCleanup.clear()
145→
146→        try {
147→            if (CollectionHelper.instance.colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
148→                CollectionHelper.instance.getColUnsafe(targetContext)!!.debugEnsureNoOpenPointers()
149→            }
150→            // If you don't tear down the database you'll get unexpected IllegalStateExceptions related to connections
151→            CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("RobolectricTest: End")
152→        } catch (ex: BackendException) {
153→            if ("CollectionNotOpen" == ex.message) {
154→                Timber.w(ex, "Collection was already disposed - may have been a problem")
155→            } else {
156→                throw ex
157→            }
158→        } finally {
159→            // After every test make sure the CollectionHelper is no longer overridden (done for null testing)
160→            disableNullCollection()
161→
162→            // called on each AnkiDroidApp.onCreate(), and spams the build
163→            // there is no onDestroy(), so call it here.
164→            Timber.uprootAll()
165→
166→            TimeManager.reset()
167→        }
168→        Dispatchers.resetMain()
169→        runBlocking { CollectionManager.discardBackend() }
170→    }
171→
172→    protected fun clickMaterialDialogButton(button: WhichButton, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean) {
173→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as MaterialDialog
174→        dialog.getActionButton(button).performClick()
175→        if (checkDismissed) {
176→            Assert.assertTrue("Dialog not dismissed?", Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed())
177→        }
178→    }
179→
180→    /**
181→     * Click on a dialog button for an AlertDialog dialog box. Replaces the above helper.
182→     */
183→    protected fun clickAlertDialogButton(button: Int, @Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean) {
184→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as AlertDialog
185→
186→        dialog.getButton(button).performClick()
187→        // Need to run UI thread tasks to actually run the onClickHandler
188→        ShadowLooper.runUiThreadTasks()
189→
190→        if (checkDismissed) {
191→            Assert.assertTrue("Dialog not dismissed?", Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed())
192→        }
193→    }
194→
195→    /**
196→     * Get the current dialog text. Will return null if no dialog visible *or* if you check for dismissed and it has been dismissed
197→     *
198→     * @param checkDismissed true if you want to check for dismissed, will return null even if dialog exists but has been dismissed
199→     */
200→    protected fun getMaterialDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
201→        val dialog: MaterialDialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as MaterialDialog
202→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {
203→            Timber.e("The latest dialog has already been dismissed.")
204→            return null
205→        }
206→        return dialog.view.contentLayout.findViewById<TextView>(com.afollestad.materialdialogs.R.id.md_text_message).text.toString()
207→    }
208→
209→    /**
210→     * Get the current dialog text for AlertDialogs (which are replacing MaterialDialogs). Will return null if no dialog visible
211→     * *or* if you check for dismissed and it has been dismissed
212→     *
213→     * @param checkDismissed true if you want to check for dismissed, will return null even if dialog exists but has been dismissed
214→     * TODO: Rename to getDialogText when all MaterialDialogs are changed to AlertDialogs
215→     */
216→    protected fun getAlertDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
217→        val dialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as AlertDialog
218→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {
219→            Timber.e("The latest dialog has already been dismissed.")
220→            return null
221→        }
222→        val messageViewWithinDialog = dialog.findViewById<TextView>(android.R.id.message)
223→        Assert.assertFalse(messageViewWithinDialog == null)
224→
225→        return messageViewWithinDialog?.text?.toString()
226→    }

228→    // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
229→    companion object {
230→        private var mBackground = true
231→
232→        // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
233→        fun advanceRobolectricLooper() {
234→            if (!mBackground) {
235→                return
236→            }
237→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
238→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).idle()
239→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
240→        }
241→
242→        /**
243→         * * Causes all of the [Runnable]s that have been scheduled to run while advancing the clock to the start time of the last scheduled Runnable.
244→         * * Executes all posted tasks scheduled before or at the current time
245→         *
246→         * Supersedes and will eventually replace [advanceRobolectricLooper] and [advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep]
247→         */
248→        fun advanceRobolectricUiLooper() {
249→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).apply {
250→                runToEndOfTasks()
251→                idle()
252→                // CardBrowserTest:browserIsInMultiSelectModeWhenSelectingAll failed on Windows CI
253→                // This line was added and may or may not make a difference
254→                runToEndOfTasks()
255→            }
256→        }
257→
258→        // Robolectric needs some help sometimes in form of a manual kick, then a wait, to stabilize UI activity
259→        fun advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() {
260→            if (!mBackground) {
261→                return
262→            }
263→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
264→            try {
265→                Thread.sleep(500)
266→            } catch (e: Exception) {
267→                Timber.e(e)
268→            }
269→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
270→        }
271→
272→        /** This can probably be implemented in a better manner  */
273→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
274→        protected fun waitForAsyncTasksToComplete() {
275→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
276→        }
277→
278→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
279→        protected fun <T : AnkiActivity?> startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(testClass: RobolectricTest, clazz: Class<T>?, i: Intent?): T {
280→            if (AbstractFlashcardViewer::class.java.isAssignableFrom(clazz!!)) {
281→                // fixes 'Don't know what to do with dataSource...' inside Sounds.kt
282→                // solution from https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric/issues/4673
283→                ShadowMediaPlayer.setMediaInfoProvider {
284→                    ShadowMediaPlayer.MediaInfo(1, 0)
285→                }
286→            }
287→            val controller = Robolectric.buildActivity(clazz, i)
288→                .create().start().resume().visible()
289→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
290→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
291→            testClass.saveControllerForCleanup(controller)
292→            return controller.get()
293→        }
294→    }

296→    val targetContext: Context
297→        get() {

300→            } catch (e: IllegalStateException) {
301→                if (e.message != null && e.message!!.startsWith("No instrumentation registered!")) {
302→                    // Explicitly ignore the inner exception - generates line noise
303→                    throw IllegalStateException("Annotate class: '${javaClass.simpleName}' with '@RunWith(AndroidJUnit4.class)'")
304→                }
305→                throw e
306→            }
307→        }

325→    /** A collection. Created one second ago, not near cutoff time.
326→     * Each time time is checked, it advance by 10 ms. Not enough to create any change visible to user, but ensure
327→     * we don't get two equal time. */

329→        get() = try {

338→    /** Call this method in your test if you to test behavior with a null collection  */

340→        CollectionManager.closeCollectionBlocking()
341→        CollectionHelper.setInstanceForTesting(object : CollectionHelper() {
342→            @Synchronized

344→        })
345→        CollectionManager.emulateOpenFailure = true
346→    }

393→    protected fun addNoteUsingModelName(name: String?, vararg fields: String): Note {
394→        val model = col.notetypes.byName((name)!!)
395→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Could not find model '$name'")
396→        // PERF: if we modify newNote(), we can return the card and return a Pair<Note, Card> here.
397→        // Saves a database trip afterwards.
398→        val n = col.newNote(model)
399→        for ((i, field) in fields.withIndex()) {
400→            n.setField(i, field)

402→        check(col.addNote(n) != 0) { "Could not add note: {${fields.joinToString(separator = ", ")}}" }
403→        return n

411→        val t = Notetypes.newTemplate("Card 1")
412→        t.put("qfmt", qfmt)
413→        t.put("afmt", afmt)
414→        col.notetypes.addTemplateInNewModel(model, t)
415→        col.notetypes.add(model)
416→        return name
417→    }

467→    /**
468→     * Call to assume that <code>actual</code> satisfies the condition specified by <code>matcher</code>.
469→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
470→     * Example:
471→     * <pre>:
472→     *   assumeThat(1, is(1)); // passes
473→     *   foo(); // will execute
474→     *   assumeThat(0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
475→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
476→     * </pre>
477→     *
478→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
479→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
480→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
481→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
482→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
483→     */
484→    fun <T> assumeThat(actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {
485→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
486→        Assume.assumeThat(actual, matcher)
487→    }
488→
489→    /**
490→     * Call to assume that <code>actual</code> satisfies the condition specified by <code>matcher</code>.
491→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
492→     * Example:
493→     * <pre>:
494→     *   assumeThat("alwaysPasses", 1, is(1)); // passes
495→     *   foo(); // will execute
496→     *   assumeThat("alwaysFails", 0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
497→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
498→     * </pre>
499→     *
500→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
501→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
502→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
503→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
504→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
505→     */
506→    fun <T> assumeThat(message: String?, actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {
507→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
508→        Assume.assumeThat(message, actual, matcher)
509→    }
510→
511→    /**
512→     * If called with an expression evaluating to {@code false}, the test will halt and be ignored.
513→     *
514→     * @param b If <code>false</code>, the method will attempt to stop the test and ignore it by
515→     * throwing {@link AssumptionViolatedException}.
516→     * @param message A message to pass to {@link AssumptionViolatedException}.
517→     */
518→    fun assumeTrue(message: String?, b: Boolean) {
519→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
520→        Assume.assumeTrue(message, b)
521→    }

536→     * Allows editing of preferences, followed by a call to [apply][SharedPreferences.Editor.apply]:
537→     *
538→     * ```
539→     * editPreferences { putString("key", value) }
540→     * ```
541→     */
542→    @Suppress("MemberVisibilityCanBePrivate")
543→    fun editPreferences(action: SharedPreferences.Editor.() -> Unit) =
544→        getPreferences().edit(action = action)
545→
546→    private fun maybeSetupBackend() {
547→        try {
548→            targetContext
549→        } catch (exc: IllegalStateException) {
550→            // We must make sure not to load the backend library into a test running outside
551→            // the Robolectric classloader, or subsequent Robolectric tests that run in this
552→            // process will be unable to make calls into the backend.
553→            println("not annotated with junit, not setting up backend")
554→            return
555→        }
556→        try {
557→            RustBackendLoader.ensureSetup()
558→        } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
559→            if (e.message.toString().contains("library load disallowed by system policy")) {
560→                throw IllegalStateException(
561→                    """library load disallowed by system policy.
562→"To fix:
563→* Run the test such that the "developer cannot be verified" message appears
564→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
565→* Run the Test Again
566→* Apple Menu - System Preferences - Security & Privacy - General (tab) - Unlock Settings - Select Allow Anyway". 
567→    Button is underneath the text: "librsdroid.dylib was blocked from use because it is not from an identified developer"
568→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
569→* Test should execute correctly"""
570→                )
571→            }
572→            throw e
573→        }
574→    }
575→
576→    /** * A wrapper around the standard [kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest] that
577→     * takes care of updating the dispatcher used by CollectionManager as well.
578→     * * An argument could be made for using [StandardTestDispatcher] and
579→     * explicitly advanced coroutines with advanceUntilIdle(), but there are
580→     * issues with using it at the moment:
581→     * * - Any usage of CollectionManager with runBlocking() will hang. tearDown()
582→     * calls runBlocking() twice, which prevents tests from finishing.
583→     * - The hang is not limited to the scope of runTest(). Even if the runBlocking
584→     * calls in tearDown() are selectively moved into this function,
585→     * when a coroutine test fails, the next regular test
586→     * that executes after it will call runBlocking(), and it then hangs.
587→     *
588→     * A fix for this might require either wrapping all tests in runTest(),
589→     * or finding some other way to isolate the coroutine and non-coroutine tests
590→     * on separate threads/processes.
591→     * */
592→    fun runTest(
593→        context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
594→        dispatchTimeoutMs: Long = 60_000L,
595→        testBody: suspend TestScope.() -> Unit
596→    ) {
597→        Dispatchers.setMain(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())
598→        runTest(context, dispatchTimeoutMs.milliseconds) {
599→            CollectionManager.setTestDispatcher(UnconfinedTestDispatcher(testScheduler))
600→            testBody()
601→        }
602→    }
603→}
604→

 51→    private var mFMap: Map<String, Pair<Int, JSONObject>>? = null
 52→    private var mScm: Long = 0
 53→    var usn = 0
 54→        private set
 55→    var mod: Long = 0
 56→        private set
 57→
 58→    constructor(col: Collection, id: Long) {
 59→        this.col = col
 60→        this.id = id
 61→        load()
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    constructor(col: Collection, notetype: NotetypeJson) {
 65→        this.col = col
 66→        this.id = 0
 67→        guId = Utils.guid64()
 68→        this.notetype = notetype
 69→        mid = notetype.getLong("id")
 70→        tags = ArrayList()
 71→        fields = Array(notetype.getJSONArray("flds").length()) { "" }
 72→        mFlags = 0
 73→        mData = ""
 74→        mFMap = Notetypes.fieldMap(this.notetype)
 75→        mScm = col.scm
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    fun load() {
 79→        Timber.d("load()")
 80→        col.db
 81→            .query(
 82→                "SELECT guid, mid, mod, usn, tags, flds, flags, data FROM notes WHERE id = ?",
 83→                this.id
 84→            ).use { cursor ->
 85→                if (!cursor.moveToFirst()) {
 86→                    throw WrongId(this.id, "note")
 87→                }
 88→                guId = cursor.getString(0)
 89→                mid = cursor.getLong(1)
 90→                mod = cursor.getLong(2)
 91→                usn = cursor.getInt(3)
 92→                tags = ArrayList(col.tags.split(cursor.getString(4)))
 93→                fields = Utils.splitFields(cursor.getString(5))
 94→                mFlags = cursor.getInt(6)
 95→                mData = cursor.getString(7)
 96→                notetype = col.notetypes.get(mid)!!
 97→                mFMap = Notetypes.fieldMap(notetype)
 98→                mScm = col.scm
 99→            }
100→    }

conversation_history.content:
The file /project/AnkiDroid/src/main/java/com/ichi2/anki/CardTemplatePreviewer.kt has been updated.

When you're done with your current set of changes to this file, you should call the read_lints tool with the specific file path and fix any newly introduced errors.

content.text:
The issue was that `CardTemplatePreviewer` did not correctly use the `ordinal` passed in the intent when previewing a card from the Note Editor. It always defaulted to `cardIndex = 0`, thus showing the first card (or first cloze deletion).

Additionally, there was a potential issue with Cloze cards where the order of cloze numbers returned by the backend might be unstable, leading to incorrect mapping between the card index (0, 1, 2...) and the cloze ordinal (c1, c2, c3...).

I implemented the following fix:
1.  Extracted the note creation logic into a helper method `createDummyNote`.
2.  Implemented `calculateIndexFromOrdinal` to find the correct `cardIndex` given the `ordinal` (handling both standard and cloze cards).
3.  Updated `setCurrentCardFromNoteEditorBundle` to use `calculateIndexFromOrdinal` to set the initial `cardIndex` based on the provided `mOrdinal` (if available).
4.  Ensured that cloze numbers are processed in sorted order by adding `getSortedClozeNumbers` extension method and using it in `cardIndexToOrd` and `calculateIndexFromOrdinal`.

I verified the fix with a reproduction test case covering both Standard and Cloze models.

403→    private fun assertTwoCards(testCardTemplatePreviewer: TestCardTemplatePreviewer) {
404→        assertThat("prev should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
405→        assertThat("next should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
406→
407→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onNextCard()
408→
409→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
410→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
411→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
412→
413→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onNextCard()
414→
415→        // no effect
416→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(1))
417→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
418→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
419→
420→        testCardTemplatePreviewer.onPreviousCard()
421→
422→        // previous
423→        assertThat("index is changed", testCardTemplatePreviewer.cardIndex, equalTo(0))
424→        assertThat("prev should be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.previousButtonEnabled(), equalTo(false))
425→        assertThat("next should not be enabled", testCardTemplatePreviewer.nextButtonEnabled(), equalTo(true))
426→    }

428→    private fun getSavedCard(notetype: NotetypeJson, ordinal: Int): Card {
429→        val n = col.newNote(notetype)
430→        val fieldNames = notetype.fieldsNames
431→        for (i in fieldNames.indices) {
432→            n.setField(i, fieldNames[i])
433→        }
434→        col.addNote(n)
435→        print(ordinal)
436→        return n.cards()[0]
437→    }

439→    @KotlinCleanup("Override fieldText in constructor and remove text")
440→    private inner class Field(override val ord: Int, private val text: String) : NoteService.NoteField {
441→        override val fieldText: String
442→            get() = text
443→    }
444→}

131→    @After
132→    @CallSuper
133→    open fun tearDown() {
134→        // If you don't clean up your ActivityControllers you will get OOM errors
135→        for (controller in mControllersForCleanup) {
136→            Timber.d("Calling destroy on controller %s", controller.get().toString())
137→            try {
138→                controller.destroy()
139→            } catch (e: Exception) {
140→                // Any exception here is likely because the test code already destroyed it, which is fine
141→                // No exception here should halt test execution since tests are over anyway.
142→            }
143→        }
144→        mControllersForCleanup.clear()
145→
146→        try {
147→            if (CollectionHelper.instance.colIsOpenUnsafe()) {
148→                CollectionHelper.instance.getColUnsafe(targetContext)!!.debugEnsureNoOpenPointers()
149→            }
150→            // If you don't tear down the database you'll get unexpected IllegalStateExceptions related to connections
151→            CollectionHelper.instance.closeCollection("RobolectricTest: End")
152→        } catch (ex: BackendException) {
153→            if ("CollectionNotOpen" == ex.message) {
154→                Timber.w(ex, "Collection was already disposed - may have been a problem")
155→            } else {
156→                throw ex
157→            }
158→        } finally {
159→            // After every test make sure the CollectionHelper is no longer overridden (done for null testing)
160→            disableNullCollection()
161→
162→            // called on each AnkiDroidApp.onCreate(), and spams the build
163→            // there is no onDestroy(), so call it here.
164→            Timber.uprootAll()
165→
166→            TimeManager.reset()
167→        }
168→        Dispatchers.resetMain()
169→        runBlocking { CollectionManager.discardBackend() }
170→    }

199→     */
200→    protected fun getMaterialDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {
201→        val dialog: MaterialDialog = ShadowDialog.getLatestDialog() as MaterialDialog
202→        if (checkDismissed && Shadows.shadowOf(dialog).hasBeenDismissed()) {

216→    protected fun getAlertDialogText(@Suppress("SameParameterValue") checkDismissed: Boolean): String? {

228→    // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
229→    companion object {
230→        private var mBackground = true
231→
232→        // Robolectric needs a manual advance with the new PAUSED looper mode
233→        fun advanceRobolectricLooper() {
234→            if (!mBackground) {
235→                return
236→            }
237→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
238→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).idle()
239→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).runToEndOfTasks()
240→        }
241→
242→        /**
243→         * * Causes all of the [Runnable]s that have been scheduled to run while advancing the clock to the start time of the last scheduled Runnable.
244→         * * Executes all posted tasks scheduled before or at the current time
245→         *
246→         * Supersedes and will eventually replace [advanceRobolectricLooper] and [advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep]
247→         */
248→        fun advanceRobolectricUiLooper() {
249→            Shadows.shadowOf(Looper.getMainLooper()).apply {
250→                runToEndOfTasks()
251→                idle()
252→                // CardBrowserTest:browserIsInMultiSelectModeWhenSelectingAll failed on Windows CI
253→                // This line was added and may or may not make a difference
254→                runToEndOfTasks()
255→            }
256→        }
257→
258→        // Robolectric needs some help sometimes in form of a manual kick, then a wait, to stabilize UI activity
259→        fun advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep() {
260→            if (!mBackground) {
261→                return
262→            }
263→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
264→            try {
265→                Thread.sleep(500)
266→            } catch (e: Exception) {
267→                Timber.e(e)
268→            }
269→            advanceRobolectricLooper()
270→        }
271→
272→        /** This can probably be implemented in a better manner  */
273→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
274→        protected fun waitForAsyncTasksToComplete() {
275→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
276→        }
277→
278→        @JvmStatic // Using protected members which are not @JvmStatic in the superclass companion is unsupported yet
279→        protected fun <T : AnkiActivity?> startActivityNormallyOpenCollectionWithIntent(testClass: RobolectricTest, clazz: Class<T>?, i: Intent?): T {
280→            if (AbstractFlashcardViewer::class.java.isAssignableFrom(clazz!!)) {
281→                // fixes 'Don't know what to do with dataSource...' inside Sounds.kt
282→                // solution from https://github.com/robolectric/robolectric/issues/4673
283→                ShadowMediaPlayer.setMediaInfoProvider {
284→                    ShadowMediaPlayer.MediaInfo(1, 0)
285→                }
286→            }
287→            val controller = Robolectric.buildActivity(clazz, i)
288→                .create().start().resume().visible()
289→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
290→            advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
291→            testClass.saveControllerForCleanup(controller)
292→            return controller.get()
293→        }
294→    }

393→    protected fun addNoteUsingModelName(name: String?, vararg fields: String): Note {
394→        val model = col.notetypes.byName((name)!!)
395→            ?: throw IllegalArgumentException("Could not find model '$name'")
396→        // PERF: if we modify newNote(), we can return the card and return a Pair<Note, Card> here.
397→        // Saves a database trip afterwards.
398→        val n = col.newNote(model)
399→        for ((i, field) in fields.withIndex()) {
400→            n.setField(i, field)
401→        }
402→        check(col.addNote(n) != 0) { "Could not add note: {${fields.joinToString(separator = ", ")}}" }
403→        return n
404→    }

412→        t.put("qfmt", qfmt)
413→        t.put("afmt", afmt)
414→        col.notetypes.addTemplateInNewModel(model, t)
415→        col.notetypes.add(model)
416→        return name

467→    /**
468→     * Call to assume that <code>actual</code> satisfies the condition specified by <code>matcher</code>.
469→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
470→     * Example:
471→     * <pre>:
472→     *   assumeThat(1, is(1)); // passes
473→     *   foo(); // will execute
474→     *   assumeThat(0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
475→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
476→     * </pre>
477→     *
478→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
479→     * @param actual the computed value being compared
480→     * @param matcher an expression, built of {@link Matcher}s, specifying allowed values
481→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
482→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
483→     */
484→    fun <T> assumeThat(actual: T, matcher: Matcher<T>?) {
485→        advanceRobolectricLooperWithSleep()
486→        Assume.assumeThat(actual, matcher)
487→    }

489→    /**

491→     * If not, the test halts and is ignored.
492→     * Example:
493→     * <pre>:
494→     *   assumeThat("alwaysPasses", 1, is(1)); // passes
495→     *   foo(); // will execute
496→     *   assumeThat("alwaysFails", 0, is(1)); // assumption failure! test halts
497→     *   int x = 1 / 0; // will never execute
498→     * </pre>
499→     *
500→     * @param <T> the static type accepted by the matcher (this can flag obvious compile-time problems such as {@code assumeThat(1, is("a"))}
501→     * @param actual the computed value being compared

503→     * @see org.hamcrest.CoreMatchers
504→     * @see org.junit.matchers.JUnitMatchers
505→     */

546→    private fun maybeSetupBackend() {
547→        try {
548→            targetContext
549→        } catch (exc: IllegalStateException) {
550→            // We must make sure not to load the backend library into a test running outside
551→            // the Robolectric classloader, or subsequent Robolectric tests that run in this
552→            // process will be unable to make calls into the backend.
553→            println("not annotated with junit, not setting up backend")
554→            return
555→        }
556→        try {
557→            RustBackendLoader.ensureSetup()
558→        } catch (e: UnsatisfiedLinkError) {
559→            if (e.message.toString().contains("library load disallowed by system policy")) {
560→                throw IllegalStateException(
561→                    """library load disallowed by system policy.
562→"To fix:
563→* Run the test such that the "developer cannot be verified" message appears
564→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
565→* Run the Test Again
566→* Apple Menu - System Preferences - Security & Privacy - General (tab) - Unlock Settings - Select Allow Anyway". 
567→    Button is underneath the text: "librsdroid.dylib was blocked from use because it is not from an identified developer"
568→* Press "OK" on the "Apple cannot check it for malicious software" prompt
569→* Test should execute correctly"""
570→                )
571→            }
572→            throw e
573→        }
574→    }

576→    /** * A wrapper around the standard [kotlinx.coroutines.test.runTest] that
577→     * takes care of updating the dispatcher used by CollectionManager as well.
578→     * * An argument could be made for using [StandardTestDispatcher] and
579→     * explicitly advanced coroutines with advanceUntilIdle(), but there are
580→     * issues with using it at the moment:
581→     * * - Any usage of CollectionManager with runBlocking() will hang. tearDown()
582→     * calls runBlocking() twice, which prevents tests from finishing.
583→     * - The hang is not limited to the scope of runTest(). Even if the runBlocking
584→     * calls in tearDown() are selectively moved into this function,
585→     * when a coroutine test fails, the next regular test
586→     * that executes after it will call runBlocking(), and it then hangs.
587→     *
588→     * A fix for this might require either wrapping all tests in runTest(),
589→     * or finding some other way to isolate the coroutine and non-coroutine tests
590→     * on separate threads/processes.
591→     * */
592→    fun runTest(
593→        context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
594→        dispatchTimeoutMs: Long = 60_000L,
595→        testBody: suspend TestScope.() -> Unit
596→    ) {
597→        Dispatchers.setMain(UnconfinedTestDispatcher())
598→        runTest(context, dispatchTimeoutMs.milliseconds) {
599→            CollectionManager.setTestDispatcher(UnconfinedTestDispatcher(testScheduler))
600→            testBody()
601→        }
602→    }
603→}

28→                .getInt(DOUBLE_TAP_TIME_INTERVAL, DEFAULT_DOUBLE_TAP_TIME_INTERVAL) * -2
29→            ).toLong()
30→    }

32→    override fun displayCardAnswer() {
33→        super.displayCardAnswer()
34→        showingAnswer = true
35→    }
36→
37→    override fun displayCardQuestion() {
38→        super.displayCardQuestion()
39→        showingAnswer = false
40→    }
41→
42→    fun nextButtonVisible(): Boolean {
43→        return previewLayout!!.nextCard.visibility != View.GONE
44→    }
45→
46→    fun previousButtonVisible(): Boolean {
47→        return previewLayout!!.prevCard.visibility != View.GONE
48→    }
49→
50→    fun previousButtonEnabled(): Boolean {
51→        return previewLayout!!.prevCard.isEnabled && previewLayout!!.prevCard.visibility == View.VISIBLE
52→    }
53→
54→    fun nextButtonEnabled(): Boolean {
55→        return previewLayout!!.nextCard.isEnabled && previewLayout!!.nextCard.visibility == View.VISIBLE
56→    }
57→}

 18→package com.ichi2.libanki
 19→
 20→import androidx.annotation.VisibleForTesting
 21→import com.ichi2.libanki.exception.WrongId
 22→import com.ichi2.utils.KotlinCleanup
 23→import net.ankiweb.rsdroid.RustCleanup
 24→import org.json.JSONObject
 25→import timber.log.Timber
 26→import java.util.*
 27→import java.util.regex.Pattern
 28→
 29→@KotlinCleanup("lots to do")
 30→class Note : Cloneable {
 31→    val col: Collection
 32→
 33→    /**
 34→     * Should only be mutated by addNote()
 35→     */
 36→    var id: Long
 37→
 38→    @get:VisibleForTesting
 39→    var guId: String? = null
 40→        private set
 41→    lateinit var notetype: NotetypeJson
 42→
 43→    var mid: Long = 0
 44→        private set
 45→    lateinit var tags: ArrayList<String>
 46→        private set
 47→    lateinit var fields: Array<String>
 48→        private set
 49→    private var mFlags = 0
 50→    private var mData: String? = null
 51→    private var mFMap: Map<String, Pair<Int, JSONObject>>? = null
 52→    private var mScm: Long = 0
 53→    var usn = 0
 54→        private set
 55→    var mod: Long = 0
 56→        private set
 57→
 58→    constructor(col: Collection, id: Long) {
 59→        this.col = col
 60→        this.id = id
 61→        load()
 62→    }
 63→
 64→    constructor(col: Collection, notetype: NotetypeJson) {
 65→        this.col = col
 66→        this.id = 0
 67→        guId = Utils.guid64()
 68→        this.notetype = notetype
 69→        mid = notetype.getLong("id")
 70→        tags = ArrayList()
 71→        fields = Array(notetype.getJSONArray("flds").length()) { "" }
 72→        mFlags = 0
 73→        mData = ""
 74→        mFMap = Notetypes.fieldMap(this.notetype)
 75→        mScm = col.scm
 76→    }
 77→
 78→    fun load() {
 79→        Timber.d("load()")
 80→        col.db
 81→            .query(
 82→                "SELECT guid, mid, mod, usn, tags, flds, flags, data FROM notes WHERE id = ?",
 83→                this.id
 84→            ).use { cursor ->
 85→                if (!cursor.moveToFirst()) {
 86→                    throw WrongId(this.id, "note")
 87→                }
 88→                guId = cursor.getString(0)
 89→                mid = cursor.getLong(1)
 90→                mod = cursor.getLong(2)
 91→                usn = cursor.getInt(3)
 92→                tags = ArrayList(col.tags.split(cursor.getString(4)))
 93→                fields = Utils.splitFields(cursor.getString(5))
 94→                mFlags = cursor.getInt(6)
 95→                mData = cursor.getString(7)
 96→                notetype = col.notetypes.get(mid)!!
 97→                mFMap = Notetypes.fieldMap(notetype)
 98→                mScm = col.scm
 99→            }
100→    }

22→fun Note.toBackendNote(): anki.notes.Note {
23→    val note = this
24→    return note {
25→        id = note.id
26→        guid = note.guId!!
27→        notetypeId = note.mid
28→        mtimeSecs = note.mod.toInt() // this is worrying, 2038 problem
29→        usn = note.usn
30→        tags.addAll(note.tags.asIterable())
31→        fields.addAll(note.fields.asIterable())
32→    }
33→}
